title: incus-apply configuration
description: Schema for incus-apply configuration files. Each YAML document in the file is either a resource definition (identified by a supported `type`) or a vars declaration. Documents without a recognized `type` value are unconstrained.

driver.description: Storage driver (dir, zfs, btrfs, lvm, ceph)

image.description: Image source for instances (e.g., images:debian/12, docker:caddy)

listen_address.description: External listen address for a network forward

network.description: Network name (for instances or the parent network of a network forward)

networkType.description: Network type (bridge, ovn, macvlan, sriov, physical)

pool.description: Storage pool name (required for storage volumes/buckets)

ports.description: Optional network forward port rules in the same shape as incus network forward edit

profiles.description: Profiles to apply to the instance

project.description: Incus project (can be overridden by --project flag)

setup.description: Setup actions to run against an instance after create, update, or on every apply

content.description: Inline file content to push into the instance

cwd.description: Working directory to use for an exec setup action

gid.description: File owner gid to set when pushing a file

mode.description: File mode to set when pushing a file

path.description: Absolute destination path inside the instance for a file_push action

recursive.description: Pass --recursive to incus file push for a file_push setup action

required.description: If false, setup failures are reported and apply continues with later setup actions

script.description: Shell script to execute inside the instance

skip.description: If true, the setup action is skipped

source.description: Source path or device for a storage pool, or a local file path for file_push setup actions

uid.description: File owner uid to set when pushing a file

when.description: When to run the setup action: create, update, or always

source.description: Source path or device for a storage pool, or a local file path for file_push setup actions

storage.description: Storage pool for the instance root disk

vm.description: Create a virtual machine instead of a container

driver.description: Storage driver (dir, zfs, btrfs, lvm, ceph)

files.description: Paths to .env files to load variables from.

global.description: If true, variables are shared across all files instead of being file-scoped.
